target.title: fix: restart gateway after isolated cron setup timeout
target.body:
## Summary
- classify isolated cron setup timeouts as a gateway-health failure class
- notify the gateway cron service when setup stalls before the isolated runner starts
- request a safe gateway restart through the existing restart coordinator so affected recurring jobs can recover centrally
- add cron-service and gateway regression coverage for the restart path

## Root Cause
Cron jobs that use isolated agent turns can fail before the runner starts with `cron: isolated agent setup timed out before runner start`. Today that error is recorded and alerted, but it is not treated as a platform-level gateway/isolated-runner health failure. Individual cron workflows can self-repair around it, but the shared failure mode keeps recurring across unrelated jobs.

## Impact
When this failure class occurs, OpenClaw now asks the existing safe-restart coordinator to restart only after the normal preflight drain says it is safe. This should reduce repeated user-visible cron failure cards without interrupting active work.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Recurring isolated-agent cron jobs were repeatedly failing before runner start while the gateway was stale/hot, producing `cron: isolated agent setup timed out before runner start` cards instead of recovering centrally.
- **Real environment tested:** Real macOS OpenClaw setup on Ghita's machine, LaunchAgent-managed local gateway on `ws://127.0.0.1:18789`, OpenClaw `2026.5.28`, Telegram/Clio plus YC YouTube recurring ingest workload active.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** I verified the PR head `853a75bbceb4413fe0fd0984fd62f014dc27c4ff`, then ran `env PATH=/Users/.../.openclaw/tools/node/bin:/Users/.../.openclaw/tools/node-v22.22.0/bin:/Users/.../.bun/bin:/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/bin:/bin /Users/.../.openclaw/tools/node/bin/pnpm test src/gateway/server-cron.test.ts src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/infra/restart-coordinator.test.ts src/infra/infra-runtime.test.ts --reporter verbose`.
- **PR-head proof that the callback requests safe restart:** The verbose test output included `✓ |gateway| src/gateway/server-cron.test.ts > buildGatewayCronService > requests a safe gateway restart when isolated cron setup times out`, and that test asserts `requestSafeGatewayRestart` is called once with `{ reason: "cron.isolated_agent_setup_timeout", delayMs: 0 }`.
- **PR-head proof that setup timeout is the trigger class:** The verbose output also included `✓ |cron| src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts > cron service timer regressions > times out isolated agent setup before the runner start callback (#74803)`, while the adjacent pre-execution-timeout regression verifies non-setup stalls do not fire this hook.
- **PR-head proof that restart churn is coalesced/deferred by the existing restart layer:** The same run included `✓ |infra| src/infra/restart-coordinator.test.ts > safe gateway restart coordinator > surfaces coalesced restart requests`, `✓ |infra| src/infra/infra-runtime.test.ts > infra runtime > restart authorization > coalesces duplicate scheduled restarts into a single pending timer`, `✓ |infra| src/infra/infra-runtime.test.ts > infra runtime > restart authorization > keeps restart requests coalesced while preparation is in flight`, and `✓ |infra| src/infra/infra-runtime.test.ts > infra runtime > pre-restart deferral check > defers SIGUSR1 until deferral check returns 0`.
- **Observed result after PR-head proof:** `test-projects` passed all selected shards: gateway `22/22`, infra `34/34`, and cron `43/43`; total `99` tests passed. This proves the new cron setup-timeout callback requests safe restart on PR head and uses the existing coalescing/deferral coordinator rather than emitting an unbounded restart loop.
- **Live recovery evidence from the affected machine:** Applying the same safe restart/update path manually recovered the stale gateway on the real machine. Live checks showed `openclaw gateway status` with `Runtime: running (pid 93467, state active)`, `Connectivity probe: ok`, `Capability: admin-capable`, and `Gateway version: 2026.5.28`. Live `openclaw status --json` showed `gateway.reachable=true`, `gateway.connectLatencyMs=45`, `queuedSystemEvents=[]`, and `tasks.byStatus.queued=0`. Live `ps` showed gateway pid `93467` around `1.0%` CPU, `3.2%` memory, RSS `534560` KB. Before recovery, the same setup had a stale gateway timeout and a gateway node process around `109%` CPU with roughly `2.8GB` RSS.
- **What was not tested:** The exact PR build was not installed into the live user's OpenClaw package; the live machine proof covers the operational recovery path, and the PR-head tests prove this patch now invokes that same safe restart path automatically for the setup-timeout class.

## Testing
- `pnpm test src/gateway/server-cron.test.ts src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/infra/restart-coordinator.test.ts src/infra/infra-runtime.test.ts --reporter verbose`
- `pnpm test src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/gateway/server-cron.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `git diff --check`

Note: `pnpm check:changed` was also attempted locally before this PR, but the local Blacksmith Testbox wrapper failed its own `--version/--help` sanity check before project checks could run.
